'''''Tag & Bink: Revenge of the Clone Menace''''' was a [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] published [[April 26]], [[2006]].

==Publisher's summary==
''Behold, the long-awaited prequel to the Tag & Bink saga! Many a Star Wars fan has lain awake at night, plagued by suspense and wondering just how this infamous Rebel pair came to be. Well wonder no more, faithful readers! Your plague has ended with this tell-all issue that caps the space-opera legend, courtesy of fan-favorite Star Wars creators Kevin Rubio and Lucas Marangon.

''The origin story of our hapless heroes begins on their respective planets, Alderaan and Corellia. These future soldiers of the Rebel Alliance are sized up for Padawan potential, and we are treated to a rare inside look at the rigorous mental and physical training undergone by aspiring Knights in the Jedi Temple! Mace Windu, Yoda, Obi-Wan Kenobi, and the elder Fett each have a role in the miseducation of Tag and Bink, but despite all this schoolin', the two decide that spying on Padmé and "the Chosen One" is a pretty good idea . . .''

==Plot summary==
{{Plot|comic}}

==Continuity==
Jango Fett's armor is incorrectly colored and instead looks like that of Boba Fett. This is corrected in the [[Tag & Bink Were Here|trade paperback]].

The title is a play on words of the titles of the three prequel movies: [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|'''Revenge of the''' Sith]], [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|Attack of the '''Clone'''s]], and [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|The Phantom '''Menace''']].

Outside the [[Galaxies Opera House/Legends|Galaxies Opera House]] are references to the musicals [[Wikipedia:The Phantom of the Opera (1986 musical)|''The Phantom of the Opera'']], [[Wikipedia:Fiddler on the Roof|''Fiddler on the Roof'']], [[Wikipedia:The Lion King (musical)|''The Lion King'']], [[Wikipedia:Wicked (musical)|''Wicked'']], [[Wikipedia:Avenue Q|''Avenue Q'']] (written in [[Aurebesh/Legends|Aurebesh]]), ''[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rent_(musical) Rent]'' (written in [[Aurebesh]]) and [[Wikipedia:Annie|''Annie'']]. The entire scene is likely a homage to [[Wikipedia:Broadway theatre|Broadway]]. Also, during the showing of [[Squid Lake/Legends|Squid Lake]], the main characters from [[Wikipedia:Finding Nemo|''Finding Nemo'']] are in the 'stage.' The [[Red Guard]]s are reading [[Wikipedia:Playbill|''Playbill'']] and [[Wikipedia:Variety (magazine)|''Variety'']].

According to Palpatine, [[Darth Plagueis/Legends|Plagueis's]] midi-chlorian manipulation caused the destruction of [[Wikipedia:Krypton (comics)|Planet Krypton]].

The characters '[[Wikipedia:Jay and Silent Bob|Jay]]' ([[Wikipedia:Jason Mewes|Jason Mewes]]) and '[[Wikipedia:Jay and Silent Bob|Silent Bob]]' ([[Wikipedia:Kevin Smith|Kevin Smith]]) are responsible for the recruiting of Tag and Bink on their respective homeworlds. In the recruitment scene Tag is levitating a [[Darth Tater]]. The [[Force detector]] being used to register Tag has a [[Wikipedia:Macintosh|Macintosh]] operating system, and has "[[Wikipedia:John Edward|John Edward]]," "[[Wikipedia:Las Vegas, Nevada|Vegas magician]]," and "[[Wikipedia:Medium (spirituality)|Medium]]" as skill levels.

In the youngling day care, a [[LEGO]] [[Venator-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Venator Star Destroyer]] (a reference to [[Erik Varszegi]]), a miniature [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], and a ''[[Star Wars]]'' lunchbox can be seen, and [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti]] mentions [[Wikipedia:M&M's|M&M's]]. Later, in the youngling school, [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] uses a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] of [[Skippy the Jedi Droid]].

When Tag is before the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]], the [[Wikipedia:Battlestar Galactica (ship)|''Battlestar Galactica'']] can be seen behind [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu's]] head. Later, Bink is seen reading ''[[Jedi Tricks for Dummies]]'', a parody of the [[Wikipedia:...for Dummies|''...for Dummies'']] series of books. Also, in the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Jedi Archives]], the [[Lost Twenty/Legends|Lost Twenty]] busts include [[Randy Stradley]] and [[Mike Richardson]], and there is a man dressed in a British suit in the background.

Outside of [[Dex's Diner/Legends|Dex's Diner]], [[Wikipedia:The Beatles|The Beatles]] can be seen crossing the street in the image as their famous [[Wikipedia:Abbey Road (album)|''Abbey Road'']] album. Inside of Dex's Diner, [[George Lucas]] can be seen filming the proceedings in a pose based on an on-set photo from [[Wikipedia:American Graffiti|American Graffiti]]. Other patrons of Dex's Diner include [[Wikipedia:Buzz Lightyear|Buzz Lightyear]] from [[Wikipedia:Toy Story|''Toy Story'']]; [[Wikipedia:Gort (The Day the Earth Stood Still)|Gort]] from [[Wikipedia:The Day the Earth Stood Still|''The Day the Earth Stood Still'']]; and [[Wikipedia:Tom Servo|Tom Servo]], [[Wikipedia:Crow T. Robot|Crow T. Robot]], and [[Wikipedia:GPC (Mystery Science Theater 3000)|GPC]] from [[Wikipedia:Mystery Science Theater 3000|''Mystery Science Theater 3000'']].

On the ''[[Jendirian Valley]]'', one of the hands raised is from a [[Wikipedia:Martian|Martian]] from the [[Wikipedia:The War of the Worlds (1953 film)|1953 version of ''The War of the Worlds'']].

During the [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|attack]] on the Jedi Temple, the bald figure running with [[Wikipedia:Aristotle|Aristotle's]] [[Wikipedia:Poetics|''Poetics'']] is [[Wikipedia:Sean Connery|Sean Connery]] as [[Wikipedia:William of Baskerville|William of Baskerville]] from [[Wikipedia:The Name of the Rose (film)|''The Name of the Rose'']].
